phrase.text: All seafarers must be conscious of the <pollution threats> to <waterways> and oceans and the serious effects that may result.
MARPOL covers <accidental> and operational oil pollution as well as pollution by <chemicals>, goods in <packaged> form, sewage, <garbage> and air pollution.

The IMO’s <Intervention|intervention> Convention affirms the right of <coastal> states to take measures on the <high seas> to prevent, mitigate or <eliminate> dangers to their <coastlines> from marine casualties.

The International Convention on Oil Pollution <Preparedness|preparedness>, Response and <Co-operation|co-operation> (OPRC) provides a global <framework> for international co-operation in <combating> major incidents or threats of marine pollution. A protocol to this convention covers marine pollution by hazardous and <noxious> substances (HNS).

The Marine <Environment|environment> Protection Committee (MEPC) is IMO’s senior <technical> body on marine pollution related matters. This Committee is <aided> in its work by a number of Sub-Committees.
